title: Testing Words That Claim to Be from God
summary: A genuine word from God carries power, conviction, and transformation.

scripture.text: For the word of God is quick, and powerful, and sharper than any twoedged sword, piercing even to the dividing asunder of soul and spirit, and of the joints and marrow, and is a discerner of the thoughts and intents of the heart.

title: Keep Your Eyes on Jesus
summary: Spiritual stability comes from focusing on Christ above circumstances.

scripture.text: Looking unto Jesus the author and finisher of our faith; who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross, despising the shame, and is set down at the right hand of the throne of God.

title: God's Word Carries Power
summary: When God truly speaks, His word carries grace, authority, and transformation.

scripture.text: So shall my word be that goeth forth out of my mouth: it shall not return unto me void, but it shall accomplish that which I please, and it shall prosper in the thing whereto I sent it.

title: Joy Is Spiritual
summary: True joy is rooted in God, not changing circumstances.

scripture.text: Then he said unto them, Go your way, eat the fat, and drink the sweet, and send portions unto them for whom nothing is prepared: for this day is holy unto our Lord: neither be ye sorry; for the joy of the LORD is your strength.

title: Giving That Costs You
summary: True sacrifice often requires surrender, trust, and personal cost.

scripture.text: And the king said unto Araunah, Nay; but I will surely buy it of thee at a price: neither will I offer burnt offerings unto the LORD my God of that which doth cost me nothing. So David bought the threshingfloor and the oxen for fifty shekels of silver.

title: Giving With Purpose
summary: Biblical giving is not random. God honors intentional and meaningful sacrifice.

scripture.text: Every man according as he purposeth in his heart, so let him give; not grudgingly, or of necessity: for God loveth a cheerful giver.

title: Giving With Understanding
summary: God desires generosity that is guided by wisdom, discernment, and spiritual understanding.

scripture.text: Wisdom is the principal thing; therefore get wisdom: and with all thy getting get understanding.

title: Intentional Stewardship
summary: God calls believers to manage resources faithfully, wisely, and with spiritual responsibility.

scripture.text: He that is faithful in that which is least is faithful also in much: and he that is unjust in the least is unjust also in much.

title: Sacred Moments Of Giving
summary: Giving can become a holy moment of worship, surrender, and gratitude before God.

scripture.text: But to do good and to communicate forget not: for with such sacrifices God is well pleased.

title: Speak To Your Offering
summary: Giving should be connected with prayer, faith, and intentional surrender before God.

scripture.text: In the morning sow thy seed, and in the evening withhold not thine hand: for thou knowest not whether shall prosper, either this or that, or whether they both shall be alike good.

title: Why Some Seeds Never Produce
summary: A lack of wisdom, obedience, and understanding can prevent spiritual fruitfulness.

scripture.text: My people are destroyed for lack of knowledge: because thou hast rejected knowledge, I will also reject thee, that thou shalt be no priest to me: seeing thou hast forgotten the law of thy God, I will also forget thy children.

title: Barrenness Is Not Permanent
summary: Seasons of waiting and delay do not mean God has forgotten you.

scripture.text: For this child I prayed; and the LORD hath given me my petition which I asked of him:

title: Capacity Comes Before Increase
summary: God often expands your character and capacity before releasing greater things.

scripture.text: He that is faithful in that which is least is faithful also in much: and he that is unjust in the least is unjust also in much.

title: Every Spiritual Transaction Has a Posture
summary: Different spiritual outcomes require different responses and alignment before God.

scripture.text: Delight thyself also in the LORD; and he shall give thee the desires of thine heart.

title: Flexibility Before God
summary: Spiritual maturity requires flexibility with God's direction and timing.

scripture.text: Trust in the LORD with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding.

title: Walking in the Spirit Requires Flexibility
summary: Spiritual growth requires willingness to change, adapt, and follow God's leading.

scripture.text: For as many as are led by the Spirit of God, they are the sons of God.

title: Make Room for What You Desire
summary: Some things must leave your life for new things to enter.

scripture.text: Remember ye not the former things, neither consider the things of old.

title: Receiving the Desires of Your Heart
summary: God gives desires through alignment, flexibility, maturity, and spiritual posture.

scripture.text: Delight thyself also in the LORD; and he shall give thee the desires of thine heart.

title: Vision Without Instruction
summary: Seeing without hearing can lead to confusion, fear, and wrong direction.

scripture.text: In a dream, in a vision of the night, when deep sleep falleth upon men, in slumberings upon the bed; Then he openeth the ears of men, and sealeth their instruction,

title: Insecurity Comes from Lack of Vision
summary: Insecurity often grows when you are unsure about your purpose and direction in life.

scripture.text: For do I now persuade men, or God? or do I seek to please men? for if I yet pleased men, I should not be the servant of Christ.

title: Comparison Reveals Restlessness
summary: Constant comparison often reveals the absence of spiritual rest.

scripture.text: But let every man prove his own work, and then shall he have rejoicing in himself alone, and not in another.

title: Examine Yourself Before Prayer
summary: Spiritual maturity requires honest self-examination before approaching God.

scripture.text: Search me, O God, and know my heart: try me, and know my thoughts:

title: God Listens to Intent
summary: God responds to sincere intention even before words are fully spoken.

scripture.text: And it shall come to pass, that before they call, I will answer; and while they are yet speaking, I will hear.

title: Purity of Heart in Prayer
summary: Pure intentions produce powerful and effective prayer.

scripture.text: Blessed are the pure in heart: for they shall see God.

title: Thoughts and Intentions
summary: God examines the thoughts and intentions behind prayer, not just the words spoken.

scripture.text: For the word of God is quick, and powerful, and sharper than any twoedged sword, piercing even to the dividing asunder of soul and spirit, and of the joints and marrow, and is a discerner of the thoughts and intents of the heart.

title: Why God Disrupts Your Plans
summary: God disrupts your plans to align you with His purpose.

scripture.text: For I know the thoughts that I think toward you, saith the LORD, thoughts of peace, and not of evil, to give you an expected end.

title: You Are Made for a Specific Assignment
summary: God created every person with a unique purpose and path to walk in.

scripture.text: For we are his workmanship, created in Christ Jesus unto good works, which God hath before ordained that we should walk in them.

title: Giving Reveals Your Vision
summary: The way you give often reveals what matters most in your heart and life.

scripture.text: For where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.

title: Vision for the Impossible
summary: God is able to do far more than what seems possible through human understanding.

scripture.text: But he said unto them, Give ye them to eat. And they said, We have no more but five loaves and two fishes; except we should go and buy meat for all this people.

title: The Danger of Self-Made Vision
summary: Many disappointments come from expectations God never gave.

scripture.text: Trust in the LORD with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding.

title: Three Types of Vision
summary: There are natural, mental, and spiritual levels of vision.

scripture.text: For we walk by faith, not by sight:

title: Vision Through Pressure
summary: Difficult seasons can help you grow, change, and see life from a deeper perspective.

scripture.text: Where there is no vision, the people perish: but he that keepeth the law, happy is he.

title: Vision Must Include God
summary: A vision without God at the center will not sustain you.

scripture.text: But seek ye first the kingdom of God, and his righteousness; and all these things shall be added unto you.

title: Moses and Delayed Desire
summary: Wrong posture and disobedience can delay or limit the fulfillment of what God promised.

scripture.text: And the LORD said unto him, This is the land which I sware unto Abraham, unto Isaac, unto Jacob, saying, I will give it unto thy seed: I have caused thee to see it with thine eyes, but thou shalt not go over thither.
